plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-330 RP10.P05.M06.S02-RP10.P06.M03.S19 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-330 descriptor-only intake coverage closes RP10.P05 fixture foundations and opens RP10.P06 permission matrix foundations across 150 planned rows without runtime permission evaluation, permission decision writes, audit event writes, AI dispatch, real fixture data, runtime tests, command execution, unauthorized data leaks, count leaks, Claude final approval, or enterprise-trust claims from local validation.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-330 is a descriptor-only intake foundation pack (150 RP10 units, RP10.P05.M06.S02-RP10.P06.M03.S19, risk C) that closes the RP10.P05 fixture foundations and opens the RP10.P06 permission-matrix foundations. Read-only inspection of contracts/intake-core-contract.json, packages/intake/src/{index,registry,service,validators}.js, packages/intake/test/model.test.js, scripts/validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs, the manifest, and the live closeout plan confirms the pack-level claim is appropriately scoped and well supported. The no-write/no-real-data boundary is enforced structurally: freezeCp330Result spreads the input first and then hard-sets every safety flag (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, loads_real_fixture_data, cross_tenant_access_allowed, etc.) to safe values, and validateCp330FixtureCloseoutRows/validateCp330PermissionMatrixRows assert descriptor-only behavior, deny-over-allow, no count/detail leaks, and no AI dispatch across all nine micro-sections. Claude is held as a read-only reviewer and explicitly NOT final approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; review-request tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/evidence packets, not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is present via forbidden_review_evidence (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled-tools, claude-final-approval/enterprise-trust claims). Distributions and range are consistent across registry requirements, contract, manifest delivery_counts, and the live plan, and CP00-330 is the current live cursor pack; tests and the validate script assert coverage.valid and foundation.valid. The pack does not over-claim: production_ready=false, status=in_progress, descriptor_verified flag pending, correctly leaving enterprise-trust closure to the human approval gate. Only two non-blocking P3 advisories noted, both reflecting the read-only review step's position in the hardening sequence rather than defects.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts referenced in manifest are not yet present (expected at review step)
findings.details: manifest.evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-330/review-receipt.json. A Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-330/ shows only manifest.json present, and the artifacts review dir contains no review-receipt.json yet. This is expected: the pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' and the hardening_sequence places read_only_claude_run_raw_capture (this review) before receipt_validate, finding_adjudication, construction_inspection, and final_closeout_validation. So these artifacts are generated after this review, not before it. Flagging only so the human approver does not interpret the currently-missing evidence files as a defect.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Ensure the closeout harness generates command-evidence.json, the normalized claude-review-result.json, review-receipt.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json before final_closeout_validation, and that final validation fails closed if any are missing or invalid.

findings.title: Validation/test green-status is verified by wiring only under read-only review; execution evidence must come from the harness
findings.details: Under the read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) constraint, this review confirms the validation and test WIRING is correctly in place to catch drift at closeout time: model.test.js (CP00-330 blocks at ~lines 668-730) asserts coverage.valid, foundation.valid, descriptor-only rows, Claude-not-final-approver, and handoff routing; validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs (~lines 490-520) deep-equals the contract against INTAKE_CORE_CP330_PACK_BINDING / REQUIREMENTS / NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and asserts production_ready_candidate and read_only. The review did NOT execute npm test, npm run validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout validators, or git diff --check. Per the authority boundary, passing local validation does not by itself establish enterprise trust.
findings.recommendation: Treat actual command exit codes captured in command-evidence.json (not this review) as the authority for green status, and keep final closeout gated on a real, non-zero-exit-free run of all listed validation commands plus git diff --check.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-330 intake P05 fixture closeout P06 permission matrix descriptor pack
